metadata.title: GEOMETRIC EARLY WARNING THEORY Exact Boundary Statistics for Bifurcation and Regime Shift Detection
metadata.description:
Early warning signals for critical shifts in nonlinear dynamical systems are traditionally based on higher-order statistical moments, specifically variance, skewness, kurtosis, and autocorrelation. While these diagnostics are useful in stable regimes, they become inherently unstable near bifurcations. This instability arises because classical moments depend on power-weighted deviations from the mean; as extreme excursions grow during a pre-shift phase, polynomial moments amplify outliers, rendering the signals noisy, delayed, or mathematically misleading.
We introduce a geometric alternative based strictly on exact boundary statistics. Instead
of estimating systemic instability from polynomial moments, we measure the topological
deformation of the occupied state-space region directly. For a continuous or discrete scalar
observable x(t) evaluated over a rolling time window W, we define the exact upper boundary
H, lower boundary L, median M, and interquartile range IQR. From these four fundamental
quantities, we construct dimensionless invariants measuring boundary expansion, attractor
drift, extreme exploration, and the loss of restoring force.
The central theorem of this framework is that approaching regime shifts first deform
the accessible geometry of the state space before they manifest as conventional statistical
instabilities. This approach yields a highly robust early-warning framework universally
applicable to turbulence, plasma confinement, ecology, finance, climate dynamics, and other
nonlinear, non-equilibrium systems.

data.title: GEOMETRIC EARLY WARNING THEORY Exact Boundary Statistics for Bifurcation and Regime Shift Detection

metadata.title: Tzolkin CRT Planting Calendars and KAM Tori Pest Population Stability Certificates for Precision Agriculture
metadata.description: Traditional agricultural calendars encode planting windows as number-theoretic intervals avoiding resonance. OmegaFlow v13 formalizes this via Tzolkin CRT ℤ₁₃×ℤ₂₀≅ℤ₂₆₀ providing 260-day base planting schedule. Calendar Round lcm(260,365)=18980 days≈52 years: proven period visiting every soil-crop-pest phase combination exactly once. KAMCheck() applied to pest population dynamics: predator-prey system passing KAMCheck() has bounded Volterra oscillations. ZPE phase entropy H<0.5→drought stress; H>3.0→waterlogging risk. κ=1.60225455 from ORCID 0009-0003-2911-444X. ZKP commitment block 40840.

data.title: Tzolkin CRT Planting Calendars and KAM Tori Pest Population Stability Certificates for Precision Agriculture

metadata.title: H³ Hyperbolic Embedding for AS-Level Internet Topology with Gromov δ Routing Certificates
metadata.description: The internet AS graph exhibits δ-hyperbolic geometry. OmegaFlow v13 computes Gromov δ from H³ trajectory providing real-time hyperbolicity certificate. Greedy routing in H³ achieves O(log n) stretch — provably optimal for hyperbolic graphs. Poincaré swarm dynamics provides distributed routing with no global state required. Fisher information distance = hyperbolic distance for Gaussian link state distributions — provably optimal for probabilistic routing. Applications: 5G core routing, CDN placement, BGP anomaly detection. κ=1.60225455 from ORCID 0009-0003-2911-444X. ZKP commitment block 40840.

data.title: H³ Hyperbolic Embedding for AS-Level Internet Topology with Gromov δ Routing Certificates

metadata.title: KAM Resonance Detection for Drug Interaction Analysis and Pharmacokinetic Chaos Certification
metadata.description: Multi-drug pharmacokinetic systems are near-integrable Hamiltonians. OmegaFlow v13 KAMCheck() certifies resonance avoidance (coprimality condition). Lyapunov λ as ADI risk score (λ<0.01=safe, λ>0.05=high risk). H³ geodesic distance between drug state vectors as molecular similarity metric superior to Euclidean Tanimoto for scale-free pharmacological networks. Tree of Life potential field provides topological map of ligand-receptor binding landscapes. κ=1.60225455 from ORCID 0009-0003-2911-444X. ZKP commitment block 40840.

data.title: KAM Resonance Detection for Drug Interaction Analysis and Pharmacokinetic Chaos Certification

metadata.title: KAM Tori and Lyapunov Certificates for Atmospheric Stability and Power Grid Frequency Regulation
metadata.description: Atmospheric dynamics and AC power grid frequency stability are near-integrable Hamiltonian systems. OmegaFlow v13 KAMCheck() provides per-block stability certificates via gcd(231,260)=gcd(231,37)=gcd(260,37)=1. Maps to: atmospheric predictability windows, power grid stability margin, climate tipping point detection. ZPE phase balance entropy (H<0.1=ordered, H>2.0=high entropy thermal) provides thermodynamic analog for grid load balance. v13 runs every block (~5s) — faster than any SCADA system deployed. κ=1.60225455 from ORCID 0009-0003-2911-444X. ZKP commitment block 40840.

data.title: KAM Tori and Lyapunov Certificates for Atmospheric Stability and Power Grid Frequency Regulation
